exercises.title: What does @SpringBootApplication expand to?
hints: @Configuration: marks as Spring configuration source
@EnableAutoConfiguration: activates Spring Boot's auto-configuration
@ComponentScan: scans current package for @Components

exercises.title: Annotate a service class
exercises.scenario: @Service marks a class as a Spring-managed business logic bean.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the annotation to make Spring manage this class.

blanks.hint: Spring annotation for business logic layer

hints: @Service registers the class as a Spring bean
Spring creates one instance and manages it

exercises.title: Minimal Spring Boot application
exercises.scenario: The smallest possible Spring Boot application.
exercises.instructions:
Write a complete minimal Spring Boot application that:
1. Has @SpringBootApplication on the main class
2. Prints 'Spring Boot started!' after SpringApplication.run()
3. Compiles (don't need to run)

Show all required imports.
hints: Two imports: SpringApplication and @SpringBootApplication
SpringApplication.run() returns ApplicationContext — you don't need to use it

exercises.title: Constructor injection — the preferred way
exercises.scenario: Constructor injection: Spring injects on the single constructor.
hints: Single constructor: @Autowired is optional
final fields: better — immutable after construction

exercises.title: @Value from application.properties
exercises.instructions:
Write a @Service that reads three values from application.properties:
- `app.name` → String
- `app.max-requests` → int
- `app.debug-mode` → boolean

Print all three in a @PostConstruct method.
hints: @Value("${property.name:default}"): reads property with fallback
@PostConstruct: runs after @Value fields are injected

exercises.title: @PostConstruct vs constructor — initialization order
exercises.scenario: @PostConstruct runs after @Value is set; constructor runs before.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @Service that demonstrates the difference:
- Constructor prints @Value field (will be null/default)
- @PostConstruct prints the same field (correct value)

Show why @PostConstruct is needed for initialization.
hints: Constructor: Spring hasn't injected @Value yet → null
@PostConstruct: all injections done → correct value

exercises.title: Spring Bean wiring — three layer service
exercises.instructions:
Write a three-class Spring Bean setup:
- `ProductRepository` (@Repository) with `findAll()` returning List
- `ProductService` (@Service) with `getProducts()` calling repo
- `ProductController` (@Component) with `listProducts()` calling service

All wired with constructor injection. No @Autowired annotations.
exercises.starter_code:
// Three-layer Spring Bean wiring
import java.util.*;
hints: Each class gets its dependency via constructor
No @Autowired needed on single constructors

exercises.title: application.properties — configure the server
exercises.instructions:
Write an `application.properties` file that:
1. Sets server port to 9090
2. Sets app name to 'flipkart-catalog'
3. Sets root logging level to WARN
4. Adds custom property: `catalog.items-per-page=25`
5. Sets Jackson to produce pretty-printed JSON

Then write a @Service that reads `catalog.items-per-page` with @Value.
exercises.starter_code:
# application.properties
# Fill in the 5 properties

# Then write the @Service that reads items-per-page:
hints: spring.jackson.serialization.indent-output=true for pretty JSON
@Value with :20 default in case property isn't set

exercises.title: CommandLineRunner — run code after startup
exercises.scenario: CommandLineRunner runs after the Spring context is ready.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @Component that implements CommandLineRunner. It should:
1. Be injected with a @Service (UserService with a loadUsers() method)
2. In run(), call service.loadUsers() and print the count
3. Print 'Application ready!' at the end

Also write the UserService.
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// CommandLineRunner with UserService dependency
hints: CommandLineRunner is a functional interface with run(String... args)
Spring calls run() after all beans are created and wired

exercises.title: Stereotype annotation equivalence
exercises.scenario: All stereotypes register Spring beans — semantic differences only.
exercises.expected_solution:
Registers: 1 (A), 2 (B), 3 (C), 4 (D)
Does not register: 5 (E - no annotation), 6 (F - @Value is not a stereotype)

test_cases.expected:
Registers: 1 (A), 2 (B), 3 (C), 4 (D)
Does not register: 5 (E - no annotation), 6 (F - @Value is not a stereotype)

hints: @Component, @Service, @Repository, @Controller all register beans
class without annotation: ignored by component scan
@Value is for field injection, not bean registration

exercises.title: Spring profiles — dev vs prod configuration
exercises.instructions:
Create three files:
1. `application.properties` — shared properties: `app.name=OrderService`
2. `application-dev.properties` — `server.port=8080`, `logging.level.root=DEBUG`
3. `application-prod.properties` — `server.port=80`, `logging.level.root=WARN`

Then write a @Service that prints which profile is active using `@Value("${spring.profiles.active:default}")`.
hints: application-{profile}.properties: Spring loads for that profile
Activate with: spring.profiles.active=dev in application.properties
Or run: java -jar app.jar --spring.profiles.active=prod

exercises.title: Unit test a Spring @Service without Spring context
exercises.scenario: Constructor injection makes @Service classes testable without Spring.
exercises.instructions:
Write a JUnit 5 test for `DiscountService.calculate(int qty, double price)` WITHOUT using Spring test context. Use plain `new DiscountService(mockRepo)` with Mockito mock.

Test: qty=10, price=100 → expects 900 (10% discount).
hints: Constructor injection means: new Service(mockDep) works without Spring
No @SpringBootTest needed — pure Mockito test

exercises.title: Full Spring Boot application skeleton
exercises.scenario: Write the complete skeleton of a production-ready Spring Boot app.
exercises.instructions:
Write a complete, multi-class Spring Boot application skeleton for a 'StockPriceService':
1. `Application.java` — @SpringBootApplication with main()
2. `StockPriceService.java` — @Service with getPrices() and @PostConstruct
3. `CacheService.java` — @Service that StockPriceService depends on
4. `PriceConfig.java` — @Component reading 3 @Value properties
5. `application.properties` — set port=9090 + 3 custom price config values

All constructor-injected. Compile-ready.
hints: All 5 classes in sub-packages of Application's package
StockPriceService constructor takes CacheService
@Value in PriceConfig reads from application.properties

exercises.title: Spring Boot test — no HTTP context
exercises.scenario: Test Spring beans by loading just the Spring context (no HTTP).
exercises.instructions:
Write a @SpringBootTest test that verifies:
1. `GreetingService.greet(String name)` returns 'Hello, Aisha!'
2. @Value property `app.greeting=Hello` is read correctly
3. The GreetingService is not null (Spring wired it)

Use @SpringBootTest without webEnvironment.
hints: @SpringBootTest(webEnvironment = NONE): loads Spring context, no Tomcat
@Autowired in test: Spring injects the bean automatically
NONE is faster than RANDOM_PORT when you don't need HTTP

exercises.title: Complete service layer with full test — Module 13.1 synthesis
exercises.scenario: Production-ready Spring Boot service layer with tests.
exercises.instructions:
Build a complete `PaymentConfigService` for a Razorpay-style app:
1. `PaymentConfigService` (@Service): reads 4 @Value properties, validates in @PostConstruct
2. `PaymentGatewayService` (@Service): depends on PaymentConfigService, has isEnabled() and getMaxAmount()
3. Unit test (no Spring): plain new with Mockito or actual values
4. Integration test (@SpringBootTest NONE): both beans wired

application.properties: payment.enabled=true, payment.max-amount=100000, payment.currency=INR, payment.provider=razorpay
exercises.starter_code: // Complete Spring Boot service layer + tests
hints: PaymentConfigService: 4 @Value fields + @PostConstruct validation
PaymentGatewayService: constructor-injected config, delegates to it
@SpringBootTest(NONE): loads context without web server — faster
